category_title: Older preschool children expressing specific verbal and emotional concerns about transition
minimal_context: The student is standing outdoors near the fence observing moving trucks and estimating how many toys can fit inside.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Look at the moving trucks coming to move the building next door. How many toys do you think can fit on one truck?

STUDENT: 600! Or maybe 1000!

TUTOR: That is a lot of toys. Those trucks and movers will move out our things in the next few weeks.

STUDENT: Will we have the same teachers?

TUTOR: We will have to wait and see.

STUDENT: But will the new school have a backyard? What is going to happen to our playground after we move?

options.A: Implement a progressive disciplinary sequence for the repetitive questions: first a warning, then a 10-minute timeout, followed by classroom suspension, detention, or school suspension.
options.B: Maintain a strict ratio of behavior-specific praise to correction at 3:1 or 4:1 during the outdoor observation activities over the next few weeks.
options.C: Integrate specialized behavioral training programs, such as the Triple E Program, BEST in CLASS, and the Behavior Expectation Discrepancy Tool, over the next few weeks.
options.D: Ask the children to help design the playground at the new location, discussing what to include, drawing pictures, and mapping out what they hope it will look like.
